title: Oil Crashes 9.6% as Iran FM Declares Strait of Hormuz 'Fully Open' to Commercial Traffic
detail: WTI crude oil plunged 9.63% on April 17 — from approximately $97/barrel to ~$85.57 — after Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced the Strait of Hormuz is 'fully open' to commercial shipping during the ceasefire period. Brent crude fell more than 11% immediately following the announcement. The collapse marks a reversal of the Iran war energy shock that had driven oil to a peak of ~$113/barrel on April 4 and contributed to the March CPI surge to 3.3% (gas +21.2%). The rapid oil price decline, if sustained, could meaningfully reduce near-term US inflation — potentially giving the Federal Reserve more room to maneuver at its April 29-30 FOMC meeting. The IEA's April 2026 Oil Market Report had previously noted Q1 2026 crude prices rose at the largest quarterly rate on an inflation-adjusted basis since 1988; the April 17 drop begins reversing that shock. Markets rallied on the news across equities and bonds.

media.caption: Oil crashes nearly 10% as Iran declares Strait of Hormuz fully open during ceasefire

title: China's Canton Fair Exposes Export Economy Risks as Middle East War Dampens Orders
detail: Bloomberg reported from China's Canton Fair — the world's largest trade fair and a real-time indicator of export orders — that exhibitors are reporting significant order declines from the Middle East market, traditionally a major buyer of Chinese consumer goods, textiles, and electronics. The fair's attendance and order data underscore March's sharp export miss (+2.5% year-over-year vs +8.6% forecast) as the Iran war disrupts Middle East purchasing power and logistics. Chinese exporters are also grappling with rising input costs as commodity prices surged in Q1, while competing with factory-gate deflation that had previously allowed them to undercut global competitors. The Canton Fair data is closely watched by China economists as a leading indicator of Q2 export momentum, and the weakness signals that China's Q1 GDP 5.0% beat may not be sustained in Q2.

media.caption: China's Canton Fair reveals declining orders from Middle East markets amid Iran war disruptions
